title: eriogonum caryophylloides
intro: Eriogonum caryophylloides, also known as Buckwheat Sagebrush, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). Traditionally, Eriogonum caryophylloides is used in herbal medicine due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, offering numerous health benefits, including wound healing, digestive health, and immune system support. Eriogonum caryophylloides can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used for medicinal purposes.
